markdown.markdown:
::: {.hero} ![](assets/VedicDateTimeLOGO2.png){width=160}

An open-source R package to compute key entities from the Vedic (lunisolar) calendar and convert Gregorian dates (or Julian Day Numbers) into Panchanga components such as tithi, vaara, nakshatra, yoga, and karana.

**CRAN:** [cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime](https://cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime) **GitHub:** [github.com/neerajdhanraj/VedicDateTime](https://github.com/neerajdhanraj/VedicDateTime) [Getting started](getting-started.qmd){.btn .btn-primary} [Cookbook](cookbook.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary} [API (pkgdown)](pkgdown/){.btn .btn-secondary}
::: ![](assets/clipboard-2026-05-11_10-09-35.png){fig-alt="Screenshot"} ## Package status [![CRAN status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/VedicDateTime)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime) ## What you get
### Panchanga entities Compute: - tithi - vaara - nakshatra - yoga - karana
### Conversions - Gregorian → JD (`gregorian_to_jd()`) - JD → Gregorian (`jd_to_gregorian()`)
### Location-aware results Many computations depend on: - latitude - longitude - timezone offset
### Time-series features Transform timestamps into Vedic features (tithi/nakshatra/yoga/masa) for modeling pipelines.
### Documentation - [Reference overview](reference.qmd) - [API (pkgdown)](pkgdown/) - [Vignettes](articles.qmd)
## Quick start ```r install.packages("VedicDateTime") library(VedicDateTime) place <- c(15.34, 75.13, +5.5) jd <- gregorian_to_jd(8, 9, 2022) tithi(jd, place) ``` ## Use cases - Calendar-aware feature engineering for time series - Research workflows requiring lunisolar calendar entities - Reproducible calendrical computations for a specified location ## Citation If you use VedicDateTime in academic work, please cite: > Bokde, N. D., Patil, P. K., Sengupta, S., Sawant, M., & Feijóo-Lorenzo, A. E. (2023). *VedicDateTime: An R package to implement Vedic calendar system*. Multimedia Tools and Applications. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11042-023-16553-w ## Key links - Getting started: [getting-started.qmd](getting-started.qmd) - Cookbook: [cookbook.qmd](cookbook.qmd) - Accuracy & conventions: [accuracy.qmd](accuracy.qmd) - API docs: [pkgdown/](pkgdown/) - Publication + PDF: [publications.qmd](publications.qmd)
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml:
::: {.hero} ![](assets/VedicDateTimeLOGO2.png){width=160}
# VedicDateTime

An open-source R package to compute key entities from the Vedic (lunisolar) calendar and convert Gregorian dates (or Julian Day Numbers) into Panchanga components such as tithi, vaara, nakshatra, yoga, and karana.

**CRAN:** [cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime](https://cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime) **GitHub:** [github.com/neerajdhanraj/VedicDateTime](https://github.com/neerajdhanraj/VedicDateTime) [Getting started](getting-started.qmd){.btn .btn-primary} [Cookbook](cookbook.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary} [API (pkgdown)](pkgdown/){.btn .btn-secondary}
::: ![](assets/clipboard-2026-05-11_10-09-35.png){fig-alt="Screenshot"} ## Package status [![CRAN status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/VedicDateTime)](https://cran.r-project.org/package=VedicDateTime) ## What you get
### Panchanga entities Compute: - tithi - vaara - nakshatra - yoga - karana
### Conversions - Gregorian → JD (`gregorian_to_jd()`) - JD → Gregorian (`jd_to_gregorian()`)
### Location-aware results Many computations depend on: - latitude - longitude - timezone offset
### Time-series features Transform timestamps into Vedic features (tithi/nakshatra/yoga/masa) for modeling pipelines.
### Documentation - [Reference overview](reference.qmd) - [API (pkgdown)](pkgdown/) - [Vignettes](articles.qmd)
## Quick start ```r install.packages("VedicDateTime") library(VedicDateTime) place <- c(15.34, 75.13, +5.5) jd <- gregorian_to_jd(8, 9, 2022) tithi(jd, place) ``` ## Use cases - Calendar-aware feature engineering for time series - Research workflows requiring lunisolar calendar entities - Reproducible calendrical computations for a specified location ## Citation If you use VedicDateTime in academic work, please cite: > Bokde, N. D., Patil, P. K., Sengupta, S., Sawant, M., & Feijóo-Lorenzo, A. E. (2023). *VedicDateTime: An R package to implement Vedic calendar system*. Multimedia Tools and Applications. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11042-023-16553-w ## Key links - Getting started: [getting-started.qmd](getting-started.qmd) - Cookbook: [cookbook.qmd](cookbook.qmd) - Accuracy & conventions: [accuracy.qmd](accuracy.qmd) - API docs: [pkgdown/](pkgdown/) - Publication + PDF: [publications.qmd](publications.qmd)
